creator: sharky@schaack.io
text:
Hello ,

as a workaround installing contextvars in

/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/ does work , but it is a pain if you don't have pip ...

I luckily had a pip in another python38 on the same machine so I could just could copy it.

From my point of view this is still a salt bug ? 

I uninstalled contextvars and it was broken again, but after 3.7 contextvars is available in python itself. 

Python 3.9.16 (main, Apr  6 2023, 01:15:39)
[Clang 13.0.0 (git@github.com:llvm/llvm-project.git llvmorg-13.0.0-0-gd7b669b3a on freebsd13
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> import contextvars
>>>

And from the contextvars readme....

The good news is that the standard library always takes the
precedence over site packages, so even if a local ``contextvars``
module is installed, the one from the standard library will be used.

From my understanding that would mean it should invoke / load contextvars from python and it should be fine right ? 

I have enabled the debugger in file salt/loader/lazy.py and surprisingly it is there...

(Pdb) contextvars
<module '_contextvars' from '/usr/local/lib/python3.9/lib-dynload/_contextvars.cpython-39.so'>
(Pdb)

Not sure what to do next...
